name: Forming Plurals
summary: Italian makes nouns plural by changing the final vowel, not by adding -s.
explanation:
Italian does not add -s to make plurals. Instead, the final vowel changes.

Masculine nouns ending in -o change to -i: il libro becomes i libri. Feminine nouns ending in -a change to -e: la casa becomes le case. Nouns of either gender ending in -e change to -i: il fiore becomes i fiori, la chiave becomes le chiavi.

The article changes too: il becomes i, lo and l' become gli, la and l' become le. Words ending in an accented vowel (città) or a consonant (bar) never change: una città, due città.
commonMistakes: Adding -s to make a plural. Italian never does this.
Changing accented or consonant-ending nouns. Le città and i bar stay the same.
Forgetting the article shifts too. Il libro becomes i libri, not il libri.
Using i instead of gli before a vowel or s+consonant (gli amici, gli zaini).
Making feminine -a nouns plural with -i instead of -e.
tip: Words ending in -co and -go often add an h to keep the hard sound: amico to amici but parco to parchi. Learn these as you meet them.
